message:
feat(rch): implement build timing history for intelligent offload gating

Implements bd-2m7j Phase 2: historical timing data for offload decisions.

The system now tracks local and remote build durations per project+kind
combination to make informed decisions about whether offloading is
worthwhile. Key components:

1. TimingRecord/ProjectTimingData/TimingHistory (hook.rs):
   - Ring buffer of last 20 samples per project+kind+locality
   - Calculates median local/remote times
   - Computes speedup ratio (local_time / remote_time)
   - Persists to $XDG_CACHE_HOME/rch/timing_history.json
   - Atomic writes via temp file rename

2. estimate_timing_for_build() now uses real history:
   - Looks up project+kind in timing history
   - Returns predicted local time and speedup ratio
   - Falls back to None (fail-open) when no history available

3. Integration points:
   - record_build_timing() called after both successful and failed builds
   - Remote execution results now include CommandTimingBreakdown
   - ReleaseRequest extended with timing field for daemon analytics
   - release_worker() sends timing breakdown to daemon

4. commands.rs: status_overview() now properly queries daemon and
   renders full status via status_display module

This enables future gating improvements where builds below a duration
threshold or with poor speedup ratios can be kept local.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
